title: 11 Herbal Essential Oils to Soothe Eczema

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for eczema because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling, and also work as an antiseptic to prevent infections, which can soothe and calm irritated skin.

Aloin is good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, making it a useful natural remedy for soothing itchy and irritated eczema patches.
Flavonoids are good for eczema because they help reduce inflammation and protect the skin from damage, which can help soothe and calm eczema symptoms such as redness, itching, and swelling.

Saponins are good for eczema because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, making them a potential natural remedy for soothing and calming eczema symptoms.
Polysaccharides are good for eczema because they help to reduce inflammation and improve the skin's barrier function, which can help to soothe and calm itchy, dry skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oil is a popular remedy for eczema. It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling on the skin. The oil also has soothing and calming effects, making it perfect for relieving itchy and irritated skin. Aloe barbadensis contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene, which are all great antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage and promote healing. It also has antimicrobial properties, which help prevent infections that can make eczema worse. When applied topically, aloe barbadensis oil can help lock in moisture, reduce dryness, and promote the growth of new skin cells. This makes it a great natural remedy for people with eczema who want to avoid harsh chemicals and artificial fragrances found in some commercial creams and ointments.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils that help with eczema, start by harvesting the flowers, leaves, and stems of the Echinacea plant when it's in full bloom. Dry the plant material in a cool, dark place to prevent it from rotting or losing its potency. Once the plant material is dry, you can use a steam distiller to extract the essential oil from the plant. This process involves heating the plant material in water, creating steam that rises and passes through a condenser, where it cools and turns back into liquid. The essential oil is then collected from the liquid. Alternatively, you can use a solvent like ethanol to extract the essential oil, but this method is not as effective and may leave behind impurities. The Echinacea essential oil has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe and calm eczema symptoms, reducing redness and itching. To use it, mix a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it to the affected skin area.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal essential oils for eczema

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for eczema because it reduces inflammation and soothes the skin, helping to calm and heal the red, itchy patches that are common with eczema.
Kaempferol is good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and itching in the skin, making it easier to manage eczema symptoms.

Saponins are good for eczema because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, making it a popular natural remedy for soothing and calming eczema symptoms.
Tannins are good for eczema because they have strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, soothing the itch and discomfort associated with the condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oil, also known as pot marigold oil, is a natural remedy that can help soothe and calm eczema symptoms. The oil is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema. It also has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infections that can make eczema worse. When applied topically, the oil can help repair and protect the skin, reducing itchiness and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties in the oil can also help reduce the severity of eczema flare-ups and promote faster healing. Additionally, the oil's moisturizing properties can help lock in moisture, keeping the skin hydrated and soft. By using calendula officinalis essential oil, people with eczema may be able to reduce their reliance on prescription creams and ointments, and experience relief from the discomfort and irritation of eczema.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils that help with eczema, start by harvesting Echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the summer or early fall when they are at their peak potency. Next, dry the flowers and leaves using a low-temperature oven (150-200°F) or by air-drying them in a well-ventilated area. Once they are completely dry, you can grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle. Combine 1 part of the Echinacea powder with 4 parts of a carrier oil, such as coconut or jojoba oil, in a clean glass jar. Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to allow the Echinacea to infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, and discard the solids. Finally, transfer the Echinacea essential oil to a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid, and store it in a cool, dark place to use topically on eczema-affected skin, diluted with a carrier oil as needed.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal essential oils for eczema

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechin is good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with eczema.
Linalool is good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with the condition.
Cineole is good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, itching, and swelling in the skin, providing relief from eczema symptoms.
Quercetin in Echinacea Purpurea is good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, promoting a smoother and healthier appearance.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, also known as tea tree oil, is often used to help with eczema symptoms. This is because it has strong anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. When applied to the skin, tea tree oil can reduce redness and swelling, which are common signs of eczema. Its antibacterial properties also help to prevent infections, which can make eczema worse. Additionally, tea tree oil has antifungal properties that can help control fungal infections, such as ringworm, that can appear on the skin with eczema. When used in a gentle skincare routine, tea tree oil can help to calm and soothe the skin, making it less itchy and uncomfortable. Many people with eczema find that using tea tree oil in combination with other treatments helps to manage their symptoms and improve their skin health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils that can help with eczema, start by harvesting the flowers, leaves, and roots of the Echinacea purpurea plant. Clean and dry the plant material thoroughly to prevent contamination. Next, use a solvent like jojoba oil or grapeseed oil to extract the plant's healing properties. Combine 1 part dried plant material with 2-3 parts solvent in a clean glass container. Seal the container and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the container occasionally to help the solvent extract the plant's properties. After the extraction process, filter the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the plant material from the solvent. Discard the plant material and store the resulting herbal essential oil in a cool, dark place. Finally, use a few drops of the Echinacea purpurea oil in a carrier oil like coconut oil or olive oil to apply topically to the affected skin, helping to soothe and calm eczema symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for eczema

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Apigenin is good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe itchy skin, making it easier to manage symptoms of eczema.

Luteolin is good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce redness and swelling in the skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita essential oil, also known as German chamomile oil, is a popular natural remedy for eczema. This oil comes from the flowers of the German chamomile plant, which has been used for centuries to soothe and calm irritated skin. When applied to affected areas, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and swelling, making it easier to manage eczema symptoms. The oil's antimicrobial properties also prevent bacterial and fungal infections, which can exacerbate eczema. Additionally, the oil's soothing and calming effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can trigger eczema flare-ups. When used as part of a comprehensive treatment plan, Chamomilla recutita essential oil can provide relief from itching, dryness, and discomfort associated with eczema.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oil, start by harvesting fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and roots. Gently clean the plant material to remove any dirt or debris. Next, dry the plant material completely, either air-drying it or using a dehydrator, to prevent mold growth. Once dry, place the Echinacea purpurea material in a clean glass jar and cover it with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. Seal the jar and leave it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow the plant's active compounds to infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the plant material. The resulting oil can be used to help alleviate eczema symptoms due to Echinacea purpurea's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This homemade oil can be applied topically, diluted with a carrier oil, to soothe and calm itchy, irritated skin.

Kaempferol is good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, and also has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica essential oil, also known as nettle oil, is a natural remedy for eczema. It's good for eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling on the skin. The oil also has antihistamine properties, which can help to stop the itching and scratching that's a big part of eczema. This is because nettle oil blocks the release of histamine, a chemical in the body that causes itching and allergic reactions. Additionally, nettle oil is rich in antioxidants, which help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Free radicals can make eczema worse by causing inflammation and damage to the skin cells. Nettle oil also has a soothing and calming effect on the skin, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that can trigger eczema flare-ups.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils that help with eczema, you'll need fresh flowers and leaves of the Echinacea plant, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a clean glass jar with a lid. First, harvest the Echinacea flowers and leaves when they're in full bloom, making sure they're free from any dirt or debris. Next, gently dry the plant material using a dehydrator or by tying it in small bunches and hanging it upside down in a warm, dry place. Once the plant material is completely dry, you can make the herbal essential oil by mixing 1 part of the dried Echinacea flowers and leaves with 2 parts of the carrier oil in the clean glass jar. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it gently every day to help the oil infuse into the plant material. After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solid plant material. The resulting Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oil can be applied topically to help soothe and calm eczema symptoms, or added to a bath for a relaxing and therapeutic experience.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale essential oil, also known as comfrey oil, has been used for centuries to help soothe and calm skin irritations like eczema. It contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling, making it a natural remedy for people suffering from eczema. The oil's antiseptic properties also help to prevent infections that can worsen eczema symptoms. When applied topically, comfrey oil can help to repair damaged skin and promote healing, reducing the risk of further irritation. Its moisturizing properties also help to hydrate the skin, keeping it soft and supple. Additionally, comfrey oil is rich in antioxidants that protect the skin from free radicals, further reducing inflammation and promoting overall skin health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils that help with eczema, first, you need to dry the Echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves. You can do this by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry, and dark place. Leave them to dry for about 2-3 weeks, or until they become crumbly. Once they are dry, you can use a steam distillation process to extract the oil from the flowers and leaves. This process involves placing the dried herbs in a distillation apparatus and heating them with steam, which causes the oil to vaporize and rise up into a condenser, where it cools and turns back into a liquid. The resulting essential oil can be collected and stored in a dark glass bottle. It's essential to note that making essential oils requires some knowledge and equipment, and it's recommended to do it under the guidance of an experienced herbalist or distiller. When used topically, the Echinacea purpurea essential oil can help to soothe and calm eczema symptoms, reducing inflammation and promoting skin healing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis essential oil, also known as soapwort oil, comes from the roots of the Saponaria officinalis plant. This oil has been used for centuries to treat skin problems, including eczema. The oil's main active ingredient, saponins, helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation. It also has antiseptic properties that prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause eczema flare-ups. When applied topically, the oil can help to moisturize and protect the skin, reducing dryness and itchiness that often accompany eczema. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce the severity of eczema symptoms, making it a potentially valuable addition to an eczema treatment plan.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oil that helps with eczema, start by harvesting the flowers of the Echinacea purpurea plant in the morning when they are fresh and fragrant. Clean the flowers thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris. Then, dry the flowers using a dehydrator or by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels and letting them air dry for several days. Once the flowers are completely dry, grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, combine the dried flower powder with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a ratio of about 1:10 (one part dried flower powder to ten parts carrier oil). Mix well and then store the mixture in a glass container in a cool, dark place for about two weeks, shaking the container every day or two. After two weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the essential oil from the solid particles. Discard the solids and collect the essential oil in a clean container. This Echinacea purpurea essential oil can then be used as a natural remedy for eczema by applying it topically to the affected skin area, or by adding a few drops to a warm bath for a soothing and relaxing soak.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oil, also known as St. John's Wort oil, has been used for centuries to help with skin issues like eczema. This oil is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm and soothe irritated skin. It also has antimicrobial properties that can help to prevent infections that can make eczema worse. The oil is also good at reducing redness and swelling, which are common symptoms of eczema. When applied topically, it can help to lock in moisture and protect the skin from further irritation, allowing it to heal faster. Additionally, St. John's Wort oil is known to have anti-itch properties, which can provide relief from the intense itching that often accompanies eczema.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils that help with eczema, first you need some fresh Echinacea purpurea flowers. You can grow them yourself or buy them from a store. Next, you need a clean glass jar, a paper bag, and some vodka or a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Harvest the flowers in the early morning, when the dew is still on them. Place the flowers in the paper bag and seal it tightly. Then, place the bag in the glass jar and pour the vodka or carrier oil over the flowers. Make sure the flowers are completely covered. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This process is called 'infusion' and it helps to extract the good properties from the flowers into the liquid. After the infusion time is over, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle. Discard the flowers and use the liquid as your Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oil. You can apply it to your skin to help with eczema.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oil that helps with eczema, start by harvesting fresh Echinacea purpurea flowers in the summer months when they are in full bloom. Choose organic and pesticide-free plants to ensure the quality of the oil. Next, clean the flowers thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris. Dry the flowers in a warm and well-ventilated area, away from direct sunlight, until they are completely dry and crumbly. Then, use a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil to extract the essential oil from the dried flowers. You can do this by mixing 1 part dried flowers with 2 parts carrier oil in a clean glass jar, and then leaving it in a warm place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. After the infusion process, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the oil from the solids. Discard the solids and transfer the extracted oil to a clean glass bottle. This Echinacea purpurea essential oil can be used topically to help soothe and calm eczema symptoms, but always do a patch test before using it on your skin.

This means it can help reduce redness and swelling on the skin, which are common symptoms of eczema. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils that help with eczema, you'll need a few basic items. Start by harvesting fresh Echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves during the summer months, making sure to choose organic plants free from pesticides. Next, dry the harvested plant material in a warm, dry place or using a dehydrator to preserve its potency. Once the plant material is dry, it's time to prepare it for infusion. Fill a clean glass jar with the dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves, and then pour in a carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil. The general ratio is to use one part plant material to three parts carrier oil. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the plant material infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. The resulting oil can be used topically to help soothe eczema symptoms, but make sure to do a patch test on a small area of skin first to check for any sensitivity.

intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a good option for people with eczema because they can help reduce inflammation and soothe dry skin. Two herbs that are often used to help with eczema are Aloe barbadensis and Calendula officinalis. Aloe barbadensis, also known as aloe vera, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm irritated skin and reduce redness. It can also help lock in moisture to keep skin hydrated and soft. Calendula officinalis, on the other hand, is known for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help prevent infections and reduce inflammation in the skin.  Eczema can make everyday life difficult, causing discomfort, pain, and itching. It can also lead to anxiety and depression. Fixing eczema issues is important because it can improve your quality of life, allowing you to participate in activities you enjoy without being held back by painful skin. By using herbal essential oils, you may be able to reduce the severity of your eczema symptoms, leading to increased confidence and a better sense of well-being. This can help you feel more comfortable in your own skin and participate in activities that bring you joy.
supplementary_best: The best combination of herbal essential oils to remedy eczema is a blend of 'Calendula officinalis', 'Melaleuca alternifolia', and 'Aloe barbadensis'. 'Calendula officinalis' oil is anti-inflammatory and soothes the skin, reducing redness and itching. 'Melaleuca alternifolia' oil has antibacterial properties that help combat infections that can trigger eczema. 'Aloe barbadensis' oil is known for its moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to hydrate and calm the skin. This combination works well because 'Calendula officinalis' oil and 'Aloe barbadensis' oil complement each other in soothing and moisturizing the skin, while 'Melaleuca alternifolia' oil helps to prevent infections that can trigger eczema flare-ups.  When using this combination, apply 'Calendula officinalis' oil and 'Aloe barbadensis' oil directly to the affected skin area to provide moisturizing and soothing relief. 'Melaleuca alternifolia' oil can be added to a bath or applied to the affected area after the 'Calendula officinalis' oil and 'Aloe barbadensis' oil have been applied. It is essential to note that 'Melaleuca alternifolia' oil can be quite potent, so it should be used in moderation and diluted with a carrier oil if necessary.
supplementary_avoid: If you have eczema, it's best to avoid certain herbal essential oils that can irritate your skin or make your condition worse. One such oil is Geranium oil (Pelargonium graveolens), which can cause allergic reactions and skin irritation in some people. Another oil to avoid is Tea Tree's relative, Cinnamon (Cinnamomum verum) oil, as it can be very irritating to the skin. Also, avoid Cajeput (Melaleuca cajeputi) oil, which is similar to Tea Tree oil and can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions.  Another oil to be cautious with is Peppermint (Mentha piperita) oil, as it can dry out your skin and make eczema symptoms worse. Also, avoid Oregano (Origanum vulgare) oil, which can be too potent and cause skin irritation. In addition, avoid Thyme (Thymus vulgare) oil, as it can be irritating to the skin and cause allergic reactions in some people. Finally, avoid Cistus (Cistus ladanifer) oil, as it can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in sensitive individuals.
supplementary_other_remedies: Oatmeal is a popular natural remedy for eczema. It works by forming a protective barrier on the skin, keeping it moist and reducing inflammation. When applied to the affected area, oatmeal can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing itching and redness. This is due to its anti-inflammatory properties and its ability to lock in moisture. Oatmeal is also gentle and non-irritating, making it suitable for even sensitive skin.  Baking soda is another natural remedy that can help to alleviate eczema symptoms. It has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to neutralize skin pH, making it more comfortable to manage eczema. When mixed with water, baking soda can create a paste that can be applied to the affected area to help reduce inflammation and itching.  Coconut oil is a rich source of fatty acids, which can help to moisturize and soothe dry, itchy skin. It is also antimicrobial, which can help to prevent infection and promote healing. Applying coconut oil to the affected area can help to lock in moisture, reduce inflammation, and soothe itching.  Apple cider vinegar is a natural antiseptic that can help to prevent infection and promote healing. When applied to the affected area, it can help to reduce inflammation and itching, and promote healthy skin. These natural remedies can be used individually or in combination with each other to help alleviate eczema symptoms. supplementary_related_ailments: People with eczema often have other skin problems. Dry skin can cause itchiness, redness, and irritation. Aloe vera is a common remedy for these issues. Its soothing properties help calm the skin and reduce inflammation.  Some people with eczema also experience allergies, which can be triggered by various factors like pollen, dust, or certain foods. Nettle leaf is a natural antihistamine that can help alleviate allergic reactions. It reduces inflammation and eases symptoms like itching and congestion.  Eczema can also be linked to digestive problems, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Turmeric contains curcumin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. It helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms like bloating and abdominal pain.  Additionally, people with eczema may experience anxiety or stress, which can exacerbate eczema symptoms. Passionflower is a herbal remedy that helps to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety levels. Its sedative properties promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.  Lastly, eczema can increase the risk of infections, particularly bacterial infections. Tea tree oil has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent bacterial growth and promote wound healing. It can be applied topically to affected areas to reduce the risk of infection.  These medicinal herbs have been used for centuries to alleviate various health issues, including skin problems, allergies, digestive issues, anxiety, and infections.
